data.title: Sanofi reports positive results for subcutaneous Sarclisa
data.description:
Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY) said that a Phase 3 study of its new subcutaneous formulation of Sarclisa in the treatment of multiple myeloma met its co-primary endpoints, demonstrating non-inferiority to the intravenous formulation.

The study, called IRAKLIA, showed that Sarclisa administered subcutaneously via an on-body delivery system, or OBDS, in combination with pomalidomide and dexamethasone, or Pd, met its co-primary endpoints of non-inferior objective response rate and observed concentration before dosing at steady state compared to intravenous Sarclisa administered at a weight-based dose in combination with Pd in patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma, according to a statement.

Sanofi added that key secondary endpoints were also achieved. The study was conducted using Enable Injections’ enFuse hands-free OBDS.

Full results from the study will be presented at a future medical meeting.

data.title: Bank of Montreal, Canadian Imperial Bank upgraded to Outperform at RBC
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets upgraded Bank of Montreal (NYSE:BMO) and Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce (NYSE:CM) to Outperform from Sector Perform in a recent note, as analyst Darko Mihelic sees a "transitional year" for Canadian lenders in 2025.

The banks within Mihelic's coverage, including (NYSE:BMO) and (NYSE:CM), are expected to boost their share buyback activity in the second half of 2025, potentially paving the way for even larger repurchases in the following year.

Mihelic wrote in a note to clients that while the outlook for this year is relatively positive, "we see several reasons for a more favorable landscape for the group in 2026."

These key drivers include "a stabilizing Canadian economy, reduced concerns on mortgage payment shock (although mortgage volumes may increase), peaking credit losses, stronger capital markets and wealth revenue growth, stabilizing capital, and buyback activity which is restarting," he explained.

Seeking Alpha's peer comparison page displays various metrics of BMO and CM.

data.title: Shein pushing for April London IPO
data.description:
Fast fashion retailer Shein could complete their £50B ($62B) London IPO as early as April, according to sources cited by Reuters, enabling FTSE-100-listed Shein shares to be incorporated into the pension portfolios of millions of UK citizens.

While the Singapore-based company pivoted from a U.S. IPO to one in London seven months ago to avoid the more rigid regulatory hurdles and political pressure in the U.S., Shein still faces challenges pertaining to concerns that the company uses slave labor drawn from the Uyghur population and sources cotton from the banned Xinjiang region of China.

The British parliament recently summoned representatives from Shein and Temu (NASDAQ:PDD) to answer questions on its labor practices, and use of banned Xinjiang cotton, but were frustrated by the company’s evasiveness. The lack of full disclosure could make a London IPO just as problematic as the one the company faced in the U.S.

“The reluctance to answer basic questions has frankly bordered on contempt of the committee,” UK committee chair for business and trade Liam Byrne was quoted by The Guardian as saying in response to Shein’s unwillingness to answer questions about its manufacturing processes.

Shein, however, hopes that an upcoming meeting between Britain’s finance minister Rachel Reeves with Chinese vice premier He Lifeng will grease the troublesome UK regulatory wheels and allow for a springtime Shein IPO.

data.title: Here is what the correlation between Q4 and Q1 means for U.S. equities – Barclays
data.description:
The S&P 500 Index (SPX) generated “unusually strong” return during the first three quarters of 2024 – compared to the long-term seasonality – before experiencing a weak fourth quarter, which lagged its 2023 performance.

According to Barclays analysts, there is a correlation between the fourth quarter returns and the subsequent first quarter’s performance.

“If fourth-quarter performance is better than the long-term median, the subsequent first quarter median return is 4.1%,” said Venu Krishna, head of U.S. Equity Research at Barclays. “When fourth quarter performance is worse than median (as it was most recently), the subsequent first quarter median return is flat to slightly negative.”

The third quarter tends to be the most volatile for U.S. equities, but last year, the fourth quarter was the weakest for the S&P 500 Index (SPX), defying typical seasonality, which usually has the fourth quarter deliver the strongest returns. The “Santa rally” is an example of that.

So, “if history is any guide, S&P 500 Index (SPX) performance could be muted in 1Q25, a sharp contrast to 2024 when equities entered the year with significant momentum,” said Krishna.

  • First quarter 2024 performance: 10.2%
  • Second quarter 2024 performance: 3.9%
  • Third quarter 2024 performance: 5.5%
  • Fourth quarter 2024 performance: 1.9%
  • Total 2024 performance: 23.31%

data.title: Siemens Mobility wins €670M of contracts for U.K.'s second high-speed rail line
data.description:
Siemens Mobility (OTCPK:SIEGY) said Thursday it secured four contracts worth €670M (~$691M) for infrastructure and long-term maintenance of a high-speed U.K. rail line connecting London and the West Midlands.

Siemens (OTCPK:SIEGY) said it will install technology enabling semi-automatic train operations for the first time on a high-speed network, and deliver an engineering management system allowing real-time control and monitoring of railway equipment.

The company also will deliver high-voltage power supply systems along the rail route and will take charge of implementing operational telecommunications and security systems for the entire high-speed rail route.

data.title: Google joins party as latest big tech firm to donate to Trump inauguration: report
data.description:
Google (NASDAQ:GOOG)(NASDAQ:GOOGL) is the latest big tech name to join in on donating to President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration, highlighting a shift from his first inauguration event.

Google donated $1M to the fund this week, according to a report today by CNBC.

Last week, it was revealed Apple (AAPL) CEO Tim Cook was donating $1M to the inauguration committee. Amazon (AMZN) and Meta Platforms (META) are each donating $1M as well. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman also pledged $1M.

This represents a shift from the 2017 inauguration, when Amazon donated $58,000 and Meta reportedly donated nothing to the event.

Google CEO Sundar Pichai congratulated Trump in a post on X back in November.

"Congratulations to President @realDonaldTrump on his decisive victory," he posted. "We are in a golden age of American innovation and are committed to working with his administration to help bring the benefits to everyone."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the discussion.

data.title: Exelixis says no FDA AdCom for Cabometyx label expansion
data.description:
Exelixis (NASDAQ:EXEL) announced Thursday that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) no longer plans to conduct an advisory committee meeting as part of its review of the company’s supplemental New Drug Application for cancer therapy cabozantinib (Cabometyx).

In August, the FDA accepted Exelixis’ (NASDAQ:EXEL) request for Cabometyx label expansion, granting Apr. 3 as the target action date. The sNDA focused on adults with previously treated advanced pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (pNET) and advanced extra-pancreatic NET.

In November, the company said that the FDA’s Oncologic Drugs Advisory Committee (ODAC) would meet in March to discuss the sNDA.

The oral antitumor agent is currently approved in the U.S. for a range of cancers, including kidney cancer, renal cell carcinoma, and liver cancer, hepatocellular carcinoma.

The Cabometyx sNDA will “no longer be the subject of discussion at an Oncologic Drugs Advisory Committee meeting,” Exelixis (EXEL) said, adding that its target action date of Apr. 3 remains unchanged.

data.title: Nevada casinos see a drop in gaming win for the fifth month in a row
data.description:
The Nevada Gaming Control Board reported that gaming win fell 4.2% year-over-year in the state during November to $1.32 billion. Downtown Las Vegas casinos saw a 2.7% decline during the month, while Las Vegas Strip casinos saw a fall of 3.9%. Gaming win was down 10.5% at Reno casinos.

Overall, gaming win for table, counter, and card games was up 0.1% to $505.9 million on a strong win percentage of 15.1% of the total amount gambled. Slot machine gaming win fell 6.7% to $810.6 million.

Nevada casinos were hurt once again by a 19.3% decline in gaming win for baccarat games during the month to $156.4 million and a 15% drop in Craps gaming win to $34.1 million. Sportsbooks shined with a 65% year-over-year increase in gaming win to $67.5 million. Football and basketball were strong during the month.

The decline in gaming win for November marked the fifth month in a row of a year-over-year decline.

Sector watch: Caesars Entertainment (NASDAQ:CZR), MGM Resorts (NYSE:MGM), Boyd Gaming (NYSE:BYD), Wynn Resorts (NASDAQ:WYNN), Full House Resorts (NASDAQ:FLL), Bally's Corporation (NYSE:BALY), and Red Rock Resorts (NASDAQ:RRR).

data.title: Enbridge wins Canada government approval for Northern Gateway pipeline
data.description:
The Canadian government approved Enbridge's (NYSE:ENB) C$7.9B (~US$7.3B) Northern Gateway pipeline to link landlocked Alberta crude supply with Asia-Pacific markets, Argus reported Thursday.

The approval was subject to Enbridge (ENB) showing it could meet 209 conditions around safety, environmental protection and consultations with local communities, including First Nations, natural resources minister Greg Rickford said.

The pipeline would carry 525K bbl/day of bitumen from Alberta to the port of Kitimat in British Columbia and then to primarily Asian markets via tankers.

Alberta Premier David Hancock said the Northern Gateway decision would create jobs and generate revenue for public services such as health care and education.

data.title: Bitcoin selloff deepens, wiping out early-2025 advance
data.description:
Bitcoin's (BTC-USD) selloff this week intensified as the cryptocurrency wiped out all its gains from the first week of 2025, with many other major token prices following suit on Thursday.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) dipped 2.4% to $93.1K at 10:017 a.m. ET, down from as high as $102.7K just two days earlier and about 14% below its record high of $108.3K last month.

The intraday slump went beyond bitcoin (BTC-USD), hitting numerous high-profile crypto assets, including MarketMove (MOVE-USD) -9.5%, cardano (CAD-USD) -8.7%, dogecoin (DOGE-USD) -7.7%, internet computer (ICP-USD) -7.1% and arbit coin (ARB-USD) -6.7%. Ether (ETH-USD), the world's largest altcoin by market cap, also slid 3.6% to $3.24K.

Coinciding with the two-day price weakness, macro jitters have been mounting as the surge in global bond yields reflect concerns about upside risks to inflation, and, consequently, a tightening in monetary policy by the Federal Reserve.

On Wednesday, minutes from the Fed's December policy meeting revealed that a majority of Federal Open Market Committee members now see slower progress on inflation, noting that December's rate cut was a close call.

The stock market is closed Thursday for former President Jimmy Carter's state funeral, but bond markets remain open until 2:00 PM ET.
